Services.metaTitle: Freelance services — Inna Boiko | React & Next.js development
Services.metaDescription: Freelance development services: React/Next.js SaaS and web apps, fast and secure frontend, maintenance, consulting and SEO. Transparent rates, available remotely.
Services.intro: React / Next.js / TypeScript developer. I turn your idea into a production product: fast, secure and built to scale. From MVP to SaaS platform.
Services.servicesEyebrow: What I can do for you
Services.s1Desc: A fast, polished website that showcases your business and lets clients book online, 24/7.
Services.s2Desc: Each client gets a secure personal area: documents, history, data and messages all in one place.
Services.s3Desc: A calendar with real-time availability, automatic confirmations and management of slots, locations and staff.
Services.s4Desc: A control panel to manage clients, bookings, quotes and invoicing — no more spreadsheets.
Services.s5Desc: Confirmations, reminders and follow-ups sent automatically: fewer no-shows and less manual work.
Services.s6Desc: Got a different need? I design and build the right solution for your workflow. Let's talk.
Services.pricingIntro: Every project is different: these figures are indicative and give you a ballpark. The final quote depends on scope, complexity and timeline.
Services.t1Note: Website, admin tool or web app with a defined scope. Price agreed upfront, no surprises.
Services.t2Note: Your first launch-ready product: Next.js, database, authentication, deploy and AI integration.
Services.t3Note: Updates, fixes, small improvements and ongoing support.
Services.t4Note: For consulting, one-off tasks or time-and-materials work.
Services.pricingDisclaimer: Prices exclude VAT. Available for part-time or full-time collaborations, remotely.
Services.step1Desc: We talk about the project, goals and timeline. No commitment.
Services.step2Desc: I send you a clear proposal with scope, price and delivery dates.
Services.step3Desc: I work in steps, with live previews and regular updates.
Services.step4Desc: I ship the product live and stay available for support.

Hero.description: React, Next.js (App Router) and TypeScript. Specialized in scalable SaaS for the healthcare sector, with a focus on performance, clean architecture and security. AI-augmented development.

Features.title: In production: ZeusDental
Features.intro: Sole frontend developer of ZeusDental, a multi-tenant SaaS platform for dental practices. Three pillars of the daily work: architecture, security and real modules in production.
Features.card3Desc: Appointment calendar Day/Week/Month/Year, quotes, invoicing and clinical data. Real SaaS modules, in production, on a multi-tenant architecture.

Philosophy.conventionalText: Too many projects chase aesthetics alone, neglecting <hl>architecture, tests and security</hl> — until they break in production.
Philosophy.manifestoText:
I start from clean architecture,

reliable tests and security

by design. AI to move

faster, not to skip steps.

Protocol.p1Desc: CRUD of companies and promotions on Next.js 16 + React 19. Parallel & Intercepting Routes, Server Components consuming typed Route Handlers, TanStack Query for server state.
Protocol.p2Desc: Server Components, Server Actions and Route Handlers. Auth.js v5 with edge middleware, Drizzle ORM on PGlite (in-browser Postgres WASM) and full SEO (Metadata API, JSON-LD, OG images).
Protocol.p3Desc: A booking platform in a single Next.js app: React UI, Route Handlers and Auth.js (persistent cookie session). Calendar with dynamic slots, favorites, IT/EN i18n and an admin moderation panel. Migrated from Strapi to Prisma + Neon: single deployment, €0 cost and no cold starts.
Protocol.s1Desc: Google Jobs API integration, an AI agent system (LangGraph) and task & note management.
Protocol.s2Desc: Threat modeling (STRIDE, DREAD), mapping to MITRE ATT&CK / NIST and definition of security requirements.

About.metaTitle: About — Inna Boiko | Frontend & Full-Stack Developer
About.metaDescription: Background, tools and education of Inna Boiko, Frontend & Full-Stack Developer: React, Next.js, TypeScript, security and testing.
About.intro: Frontend & Full-Stack Developer based in Bari. I bring real experience with production SaaS, continuous learning and an approach built on clean architecture, testing and security.
About.bio1: I'm Inna Boiko, a Frontend & Full-Stack Developer based in Bari, Italy. I build web products with React, Next.js and TypeScript — from architecture to deployment, with a focus on performance, security and testable code.
About.bio2: I work as the sole frontend developer of ZeusDental, a multi-tenant SaaS platform in the healthcare sector, in production since November 2024. This experience taught me to make architectural decisions autonomously, handle the security of sensitive health data and maintain a suite of over 3,700 automated tests.
About.bio3: I hold a degree in Economics and Cybernetics and am completing a Master's in Computer Science (Software Engineering & AI) at Neoversity / IT University. I integrate AI tools into my daily workflow — not to skip steps, but to move faster while maintaining quality and control over the code.

CaseStudies.metaTitle: Case studies — Inna Boiko | Next.js & React in production
CaseStudies.metaDescription: Real case studies: ZeusDental multi-tenant SaaS, Psychologists Services, Living Notebook and CRM Dashboard. Advanced Next.js architectures, TypeScript and security.
CaseStudies.indexIntro: Not just what I built, but how and why. Context, technical challenges and results for every project.
CaseStudies.ctaText: I can build clean, secure, production-ready architectures for you. Let's talk.

zeusdental.title: ZeusDental
zeusdental.subtitle: Multi-tenant SaaS platform for dental clinics
zeusdental.summary: Frontend developer of a healthcare platform in production: feature-slice SPA architecture, security-by-design and real SaaS modules (appointments, quotes, invoicing).
zeusdental.context: ZeusDental is a multi-tenant SaaS platform for managing dental clinics: appointments, clinical data, quotes and invoicing, with multiple clinics served by the same application. I own the product's entire frontend, from SPA architecture to production releases.
zeusdental.challenge: Handling sensitive medical data demands non-negotiable security and reliability. The challenge: build a scalable, multi-tenant SPA with data isolation between clinics, robust frontend security and complex modules (calendar, invoicing) while keeping the code testable and maintainable by a single person.
zeusdental.solutionIntro: I structured the work around three pillars: architecture, security and real production modules.
zeusdental.solArchDesc: React SPA organized into 13 feature slices, Redux Toolkit + redux-persist for state, React Router 7 and a clinic-context to isolate each tenant's data.
zeusdental.solSecDesc: Encrypted JWT (AES-GCM 256-bit, Web Crypto API), Content Security Policy, inactivity timer, cross-tab logout via BroadcastChannel and applied STRIDE threat modeling.
zeusdental.solModDesc: Day/Week/Month/Year appointment calendar, quotes, invoicing and clinical data: real modules, in production, on a multi-tenant architecture.
zeusdental.result: The platform is in production and used daily by clinics. The codebase is covered by a reliable test suite that protects evolutions and releases.

psychologists-services.title: Psychologists Services
psychologists-services.subtitle: Full-stack booking platform for psychologists
psychologists-services.summary: Full-stack platform migrated from Next.js + Strapi (two deployments) to a Next.js 14 monolith with Prisma 5 and Neon. Auth.js v5 with persistent cookie sessions, GDPR compliant, IT/EN i18n and GitHub Actions CI.
psychologists-services.context: A demo platform connecting patients with certified psychologists: filters by specialization, favorites, dynamic appointment calendar and an admin dashboard for profile approval. The original version used Strapi as a separate backend on Strapi Cloud, with 30-second cold starts and two deployments to manage.
psychologists-services.challenge: The Strapi backend on Strapi Cloud took up to 30 seconds to restart after inactivity, required an external keep-alive service and complicated deployments. JWT sessions didn't persist across browser reloads. A monolithic architecture with zero cold starts and €0 running cost was needed.
psychologists-services.solutionIntro: Migration to a Next.js monolith built on three pillars: architecture, security and UX.
psychologists-services.solArchDesc: From Strapi + Strapi Cloud to a single Next.js 14 app on Vercel. Route Handlers replace Strapi controllers. Prisma 5 on Neon (serverless PostgreSQL) — resumes in ~500 ms, €0 cost. Single deployment, zero separate infrastructure.
psychologists-services.solSecDesc: Auth.js v5 with HttpOnly cookie sessions (bcryptjs). Rate limiting on auth endpoints via Upstash Redis. GDPR account deletion: cascades across user, bookings and profile. Explicit consent, Privacy Policy, cookie banner.
psychologists-services.solUXDesc: Calendar with dynamic slots based on each practitioner's working hours. Name and specialization filters. Persistent favorites list. Transactional emails with Resend. IT/EN i18n. Accessibility: ARIA, keyboard navigation, prefers-reduced-motion.
psychologists-services.result: A single Vercel deployment eliminates cold starts, reduces running costs to €0 and simplifies maintenance. Sessions persist across reloads. The project demonstrates a real migration from a two-tier architecture to a Next.js monolith.

living-notebook.title: Living Notebook
living-notebook.subtitle: Interactive educational platform on Next.js 16 and React 19
living-notebook.summary: Educational app where each Next.js route is a runnable hands-on lesson. 20 lessons in 5 modules: App Router, data fetching, auth, performance and deployment. TypeScript 100%, Edge-safe Auth.js v5, PGlite WASM and an 80 MB Docker standalone image.
living-notebook.context: An interactive notebook for learning the Next.js App Router by reading source code and running real examples in the browser. 20 lessons across 5 progressive modules: routing and Server Components, data fetching, client state, full-stack APIs and auth, performance and deployment. Designed so learners understand how things actually work.
living-notebook.challenge: Three main technical challenges: making Auth.js v5 compatible with the Edge runtime without crashes from Node-only dependencies; providing a local PostgreSQL database without extra installations to lower the barrier to entry; maintaining 100% TypeScript with zero any across a full-stack codebase.
living-notebook.solutionIntro: Three pillars: Edge-safe Auth split config, zero-install WASM database and security by design.
living-notebook.solArchDesc: Auth.js v5 split into auth.config.ts (Edge-safe, no bcrypt or DB) and auth.ts (full Node config with Credentials provider, Drizzle adapter and bcrypt). The proxy middleware imports only the Edge-safe version, preventing crashes from Node-only dependencies at the Edge runtime.
living-notebook.solDBDesc: PGlite runs PostgreSQL in-process via WebAssembly: no local PostgreSQL to install, ideal for hands-on lessons. Docker standalone output: ~80 MB vs ~800 MB with full node_modules. 15 of 20 lessons are statically prerendered.
living-notebook.solSecDesc: Per-request CSP nonces generated at the Edge. HMAC-signed webhooks with timingSafeEqual (timing-attack safe). HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Permissions-Policy. Partial prerendering with Suspense. Environment variables validated with Zod at boot.
living-notebook.result: A platform that teaches advanced Next.js by showing every pattern in real, runnable code. Zero setup for anyone who clones it: no database to install, no mandatory external services.

crm-dashboard.title: CRM Dashboard
crm-dashboard.subtitle: CRM dashboard with Parallel and Intercepting Routes
crm-dashboard.summary: Full-stack CRM dashboard for sales teams. Company and promotion management on Next.js 16 with independent Parallel Routes, Intercepting Routes for modals with real URLs, Drizzle ORM on Neon and TanStack Query 5.
crm-dashboard.context: A CRM dashboard for sales teams: company tracking, promotion management and performance monitoring with Admin (full CRUD) and User (read-only) roles. Real-time metrics, geographic distribution across 6 markets and 8 categorized sectors. Each dashboard section loads independently.
crm-dashboard.challenge: The main challenge: editing forms that are both fast (modal, no full navigation) and navigable via a direct, bookmarkable URL. Dashboard sections must isolate errors — one failing widget must not block the rest. Normalized DB schema but simple denormalized API responses for the frontend.
crm-dashboard.solutionIntro: Three advanced Next.js App Router patterns combined together.
crm-dashboard.solRoutesDesc: Each slot (@slot) loads, errors and streams independently via Parallel Routes. Intercepting Routes open edit forms as modals with bookmarkable URLs: direct URL access falls back to the full page. No compromise between UX speed and link shareability.
crm-dashboard.solDataDesc: Normalized schema with Drizzle ORM on Neon PostgreSQL (companies, categories, countries, promotions as separate tables). TanStack Query 5 for client-side server state caching. TypeScript-typed Route Handlers for end-to-end type safety.
crm-dashboard.solTestDesc: Vitest 4 + React Testing Library for unit and component tests. TypeScript 99.2% across the entire codebase, zero any. Client-side mock auth for role testing without a real auth infrastructure in tests.
crm-dashboard.result: A working dashboard demonstrating advanced Next.js 16 App Router patterns in a real business context: parallel routing, intercepting routes and end-to-end typed data fetching with TypeScript.

Privacy.metaDescription: Privacy policy under GDPR: data collected, purposes and your rights.
Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.s1Body: Inna Boiko, Frontend & Full-Stack Developer — Bari, Italy. Email: inna_boiko@libero.it
Privacy.s2Body: Through the contact form I collect name, email address and message. I do not use tracking cookies, install third-party trackers or collect browsing data.
Privacy.s3Body: Data is processed solely to respond to your contact request (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR — processing necessary for pre-contractual measures at the request of the data subject).
Privacy.s4Body: Messages are delivered via Web3Forms (web3forms.com), acting as data processor. I do not share your data with any other party.
Privacy.s5Body: Data is retained for as long as needed to respond to your request and then deleted.
Privacy.s6Body: Under Arts. 15–22 GDPR you have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict, port or object to processing of your data. To exercise these rights write to inna_boiko@libero.it.
Privacy.s7Body: You have the right to lodge a complaint with the Garante per la Protezione dei Dati Personali (garanteprivacy.it).
Privacy.s8Body: This site does not use tracking cookies or third-party trackers. The only technical cookies are those set by next-intl to store your preferred language.
